14	
15	// Description: A mailbox with two AXI4-Lite slave ports and associated interrupt requests.
16	//              See `doc/axi_lite_mailbox.md` for the documentation, including the definition
17	//              of parameters and ports.
18	
19	`include "common_cells/registers.svh"
20	
21	module axi_lite_mailbox #(
22	  parameter int unsigned MailboxDepth = 32'd0,
23	  parameter bit unsigned IrqEdgeTrig  = 1'b0,
24	  parameter bit unsigned IrqActHigh   = 1'b1,
25	  parameter int unsigned AxiAddrWidth = 32'd0,
26	  parameter int unsigned AxiDataWidth = 32'd0,
27	  parameter type         req_lite_t   = logic,
28	  parameter type         resp_lite_t  = logic,
29	  // DEPENDENT PARAMETERS, DO NOT OVERRIDE!
30	  parameter type         addr_t       = logic [AxiAddrWidth-1:0]
31	) (
32	  input  logic             clk_i,       // Clock
33	  input  logic             rst_ni,      // Asynchronous reset active low
34	  input  logic             test_i,      // Testmode enable
35	  // slave ports [1:0]
36	  input  req_lite_t  [1:0] slv_reqs_i,
37	  output resp_lite_t [1:0] slv_resps_o,
38	  output logic       [1:0] irq_o,       // interrupt output for each port
39	  input  addr_t      [1:0] base_addr_i  // base address for each port
40	);
41	  localparam int unsigned FifoUsageWidth = $clog2(MailboxDepth);
42	  typedef logic [AxiDataWidth-1:0] data_t;
43	  // usage type of the mailbox FIFO, also the type of the threshold comparison
44	  // is one bit wider, MSB is the fifo_full flag of the respective fifo
45	  typedef logic [FifoUsageWidth:0] usage_t;
46	
47	  // signal declaration for the mailbox FIFO's, signal index is the port
48	  logic   [1:0] mbox_full,    mbox_empty;   // index is the instantiated mailbox FIFO
49	  logic   [1:0] mbox_push,    mbox_pop;     // index is port
50	  logic   [1:0] w_mbox_flush, r_mbox_flush; // index is port
51	  data_t  [1:0] mbox_w_data,  mbox_r_data;  // index is port
52	  usage_t [1:0] mbox_usage;                 // index is the instantiated mailbox FIFO
53	  // interrupt request from this slave port, level triggered, active high --> convert
54	  logic   [1:0] slv_irq;
55	  logic   [1:0] clear_irq;
56	
57	  axi_lite_mailbox_slave #(
58	    .MailboxDepth ( MailboxDepth ),
59	    .AxiAddrWidth ( AxiAddrWidth ),
60	    .AxiDataWidth ( AxiDataWidth ),
61	    .req_lite_t   ( req_lite_t   ),
62	    .resp_lite_t  ( resp_lite_t  ),
63	    .addr_t       ( addr_t       ),
64	    .data_t       ( data_t       ),
65	    .usage_t      ( usage_t      )  // fill pointer from MBOX FIFO
66	  ) i_slv_port_0 (
67	    .clk_i,   // Clock
68	    .rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
69	    // slave port
70	    .slv_req_i      ( slv_reqs_i[0]   ),
71	    .slv_resp_o     ( slv_resps_o[0]  ),
72	    .base_addr_i    ( base_addr_i[0]  ), // base address for the slave port
73	    // write FIFO port
74	    .mbox_w_data_o  ( mbox_w_data[0]  ),
75	    .mbox_w_full_i  ( mbox_full[0]    ),
76	    .mbox_w_push_o  ( mbox_push[0]    ),
77	    .mbox_w_flush_o ( w_mbox_flush[0] ),
78	    .mbox_w_usage_i ( mbox_usage[0]   ),
79	    // read FIFO port
80	    .mbox_r_data_i  ( mbox_r_data[0]  ),
81	    .mbox_r_empty_i ( mbox_empty[1]   ),
82	    .mbox_r_pop_o   ( mbox_pop[0]     ),
83	    .mbox_r_flush_o ( r_mbox_flush[0] ),
84	    .mbox_r_usage_i ( mbox_usage[1]   ),
85	    // interrupt output, level triggered, active high, conversion in top
86	    .irq_o          ( slv_irq[0]      ),
87	    .clear_irq_o    ( clear_irq[0]    )
88	  );
89	
90	  axi_lite_mailbox_slave #(
91	    .MailboxDepth ( MailboxDepth ),
92	    .AxiAddrWidth ( AxiAddrWidth ),
93	    .AxiDataWidth ( AxiDataWidth ),
94	    .req_lite_t   ( req_lite_t   ),
95	    .resp_lite_t  ( resp_lite_t  ),
96	    .addr_t       ( addr_t       ),
97	    .data_t       ( data_t       ),
98	    .usage_t      ( usage_t      )  // fill pointer from MBOX FIFO
99	  ) i_slv_port_1 (
100	    .clk_i,   // Clock
101	    .rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
102	    // slave port
103	    .slv_req_i      ( slv_reqs_i[1]   ),
104	    .slv_resp_o     ( slv_resps_o[1]  ),
105	    .base_addr_i    ( base_addr_i[1]  ), // base address for the slave port
106	    // write FIFO port
107	    .mbox_w_data_o  ( mbox_w_data[1]  ),
108	    .mbox_w_full_i  ( mbox_full[1]    ),
109	    .mbox_w_push_o  ( mbox_push[1]    ),
110	    .mbox_w_flush_o ( w_mbox_flush[1] ),
111	    .mbox_w_usage_i ( mbox_usage[1]   ),
112	    // read FIFO port
113	    .mbox_r_data_i  ( mbox_r_data[1]  ),
114	    .mbox_r_empty_i ( mbox_empty[0]   ),
115	    .mbox_r_pop_o   ( mbox_pop[1]     ),
116	    .mbox_r_flush_o ( r_mbox_flush[1] ),
117	    .mbox_r_usage_i ( mbox_usage[0]   ),
118	    // interrupt output, level triggered, active high, conversion in top
119	    .irq_o          ( slv_irq[1]      ),
120	    .clear_irq_o    ( clear_irq[1]    )
121	  );
122	
123	  // the usage gets concatinated with the full flag to have consistent threshold detection
124	  logic [FifoUsageWidth-1:0] mbox_0_to_1_usage, mbox_1_to_0_usage;
125	  fifo_v3 #(
126	    .FALL_THROUGH ( 1'b0         ),
127	    .DEPTH        ( MailboxDepth ),
128	    .dtype        ( data_t       )
129	  ) i_mbox_0_to_1 (
130	    .clk_i,
131	    .rst_ni,
132	    .testmode_i( test_i                            ),
133	    .flush_i   ( w_mbox_flush[0] | r_mbox_flush[1] ),
134	    .full_o    ( mbox_full[0]                      ),
135	    .empty_o   ( mbox_empty[0]                     ),
136	    .usage_o   ( mbox_0_to_1_usage                 ),
137	    .data_i    ( mbox_w_data[0]                    ),
138	    .push_i    ( mbox_push[0]                      ),
139	    .data_o    ( mbox_r_data[1]                    ),
140	    .pop_i     ( mbox_pop[1]                       )
141	  );
142	  // assign the MSB of the FIFO to the correct usage signal
143	  assign mbox_usage[0] = {mbox_full[0], mbox_0_to_1_usage};
144	
145	  fifo_v3 #(
146	    .FALL_THROUGH ( 1'b0         ),
147	    .DEPTH        ( MailboxDepth ),
148	    .dtype        ( data_t       )
149	  ) i_mbox_1_to_0 (
150	    .clk_i,
151	    .rst_ni,
152	    .testmode_i( test_i                            ),
153	    .flush_i   ( w_mbox_flush[1] | r_mbox_flush[0] ),
154	    .full_o    ( mbox_full[1]                      ),
155	    .empty_o   ( mbox_empty[1]                     ),
156	    .usage_o   ( mbox_1_to_0_usage                 ),
157	    .data_i    ( mbox_w_data[1]                    ),
158	    .push_i    ( mbox_push[1]                      ),
159	    .data_o    ( mbox_r_data[0]                    ),
160	    .pop_i     ( mbox_pop[0]                       )
161	  );
162	  assign mbox_usage[1] = {mbox_full[1], mbox_1_to_0_usage};
163	
164	  for (genvar i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin : gen_irq_conversion
165	    if (IrqEdgeTrig) begin : gen_irq_edge
166	      logic irq_q, irq_d, update_irq;
167	
168	      always_comb begin
169	        // default assignments
170	        irq_d      = irq_q;
171	        update_irq = 1'b0;
172	        // init the irq and pulse only on update
173	        irq_o[i]   = ~IrqActHigh;
174	        if (clear_irq[i]) begin
175	          irq_d      = 1'b0;
176	          update_irq = 1'b1;
177	        end else if (!irq_q && slv_irq[i]) begin
178	          irq_d      = 1'b1;
179	          update_irq = 1'b1;
180	          irq_o[i]   = IrqActHigh; // on update of the register pulse the irq signal
181	        end
182	      end
183	
184	      `FFLARN(irq_q, irq_d, update_irq, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
185	    end else begin : gen_irq_level
186	      assign irq_o[i] = (IrqActHigh) ? slv_irq[i] : ~slv_irq[i];
187	    end
188	  end
189	
190	  // pragma translate_off
191	  `ifndef VERILATOR
192	  initial begin : proc_check_params
193	    mailbox_depth:  assert (MailboxDepth > 1) else $fatal(1, "MailboxDepth has to be at least 2");
194	    axi_addr_width: assert (AxiAddrWidth > 0) else $fatal(1, "AxiAddrWidth has to be > 0");
195	    axi_data_width: assert (AxiDataWidth > 0) else $fatal(1, "AxiDataWidth has to be > 0");
196	  end
197	  `endif
198	  // pragma translate_on
199	endmodule
200	
201	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
202	
203	// slave port module
204	module axi_lite_mailbox_slave #(
205	  parameter int unsigned MailboxDepth = 32'd16,
206	  parameter int unsigned AxiAddrWidth = 32'd32,
207	  parameter int unsigned AxiDataWidth = 32'd32,
208	  parameter type         req_lite_t   = logic,
209	  parameter type         resp_lite_t  = logic,
210	  parameter type         addr_t       = logic [AxiAddrWidth-1:0],
211	  parameter type         data_t       = logic [AxiDataWidth-1:0],
212	  parameter type         usage_t      = logic                     // fill pointer from MBOX FIFO
213	) (
214	  input  logic       clk_i,   // Clock
215	  input  logic       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
216	  // slave port
217	  input  req_lite_t  slv_req_i,
218	  output resp_lite_t slv_resp_o,
219	  input  addr_t      base_addr_i, // base address for the slave port
220	  // write FIFO port
221	  output data_t      mbox_w_data_o,
222	  input  logic       mbox_w_full_i,
223	  output logic       mbox_w_push_o,
224	  output logic       mbox_w_flush_o,
225	  input  usage_t     mbox_w_usage_i,
226	  // read FIFO port
227	  input  data_t      mbox_r_data_i,
228	  input  logic       mbox_r_empty_i,
229	  output logic       mbox_r_pop_o,
230	  output logic       mbox_r_flush_o,
231	  input  usage_t     mbox_r_usage_i,
232	  // interrupt output, level triggered, active high, conversion in top
233	  output logic       irq_o,
234	  output logic       clear_irq_o // clear the edge trigger irq register in `axi_lite_mailbox`
235	);
236	
237	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_lite_t)
238	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(r_chan_lite_t, data_t)
239	
240	  localparam int unsigned NoRegs = 32'd10;
241	  typedef enum logic [3:0] {
242	    MBOXW  = 4'd0, // Mailbox write register
243	    MBOXR  = 4'd1, // Mailbox read register
244	    STATUS = 4'd2, // Mailbox status register
245	    ERROR  = 4'd3, // Mailbox error register
246	    WIRQT  = 4'd4, // Write interrupt request threshold register
247	    RIRQT  = 4'd5, // Read interrupt request threshold register
248	    IRQS   = 4'd6, // Interrupt request status register
249	    IRQEN  = 4'd7, // Interrupt request enable register
250	    IRQP   = 4'd8, // Interrupt request pending register
251	    CTRL   = 4'd9  // Mailbox control register
252	  } reg_e;
253	  // address map rule struct, as required from `addr_decode` from `common_cells`
254	  typedef struct packed {
255	    int unsigned idx;
256	    addr_t       start_addr;
257	    addr_t       end_addr;
258	  } rule_t;
259	  // output type of the address decoders, to be casted onto the enum type `reg_e`
260	  typedef logic [$clog2(NoRegs)-1:0] idx_t;
261	
262	  // LITE response signals, go into the output spill registers to prevent combinational response
263	  logic         b_valid, b_ready;
264	  b_chan_lite_t b_chan;
265	  logic         r_valid, r_ready;
266	  r_chan_lite_t r_chan;
267	  // address map generation
268	  rule_t [NoRegs-1:0] addr_map;
269	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NoRegs; i++) begin : gen_addr_map
270	    assign addr_map[i] = '{
271	        idx:        i,
272	        start_addr: base_addr_i +  i      * (AxiDataWidth / 8),
273	        end_addr:   base_addr_i + (i + 1) * (AxiDataWidth / 8),
274	        default:    '0
275	    };
276	  end
277	  // address decode flags
278	  idx_t w_reg_idx,   r_reg_idx;
279	  logic dec_w_valid, dec_r_valid;
280	
281	  // mailbox register signal declarations, get extended when read, some of these regs
282	  // are build combinationally, indicated by the absence of the `*_d` signal
283	
284	  logic [3:0] status_q;           // mailbox status register (read only)
285	  logic [1:0] error_q,   error_d; // mailbox error register
286	  data_t      wirqt_q,   wirqt_d; // write interrupt request threshold register
287	  data_t      rirqt_q,   rirqt_d; // read interrupt request threshold register
288	  logic [2:0] irqs_q,    irqs_d;  // interrupt request status register
289	  logic [2:0] irqen_q,   irqen_d; // interrupt request enable register
290	  logic [2:0] irqp_q;             // interrupt request pending register (read only)
291	  logic [1:0] ctrl_q;             // mailbox control register
292	  logic       update_regs;        // register enable signal
293	
294	  // register instantiation
295	  `FFLARN(error_q, error_d, update_regs, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
296	  `FFLARN(wirqt_q, wirqt_d, update_regs, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
297	  `FFLARN(rirqt_q, rirqt_d, update_regs, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
298	  `FFLARN(irqs_q, irqs_d, update_regs, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
299	  `FFLARN(irqen_q, irqen_d, update_regs, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
300	
301	  // Mailbox FIFO data assignments
302	  for (genvar i = 0; i < (AxiDataWidth/8); i++) begin : gen_w_mbox_data
303	    assign mbox_w_data_o[i*8+:8] = slv_req_i.w.strb[i] ? slv_req_i.w.data[i*8+:8] : '0;
304	  end
305	
306	  // combinational mailbox register assignments, for the read only registers
307	  assign status_q = { mbox_r_usage_i > usage_t'(rirqt_q),
308	                      mbox_w_usage_i > usage_t'(wirqt_q),
309	                      mbox_w_full_i,
310	                      mbox_r_empty_i };
311	  assign irqp_q   = irqs_q & irqen_q;                 // interrupt request pending is bit wise and
312	  assign ctrl_q   = {mbox_r_flush_o, mbox_w_flush_o}; // read ctrl_q is flush signals
313	  assign irq_o    = |irqp_q;                          // generate an active-high level irq
314	
315	  always_comb begin
316	    // slave port channel outputs for the AW, W and R channel, other driven from spill register
317	    slv_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
318	    slv_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
319	    b_chan              = '{resp: axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR};
320	    b_valid             = 1'b0;
321	    slv_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
322	    r_chan              = '{data: '0, resp: axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR};
323	    r_valid             = 1'b0;
324	    // Default assignments for the internal registers
325	    error_d     = error_q;   // mailbox error register
326	    wirqt_d     = wirqt_q;   // write interrupt request threshold register
327	    rirqt_d     = rirqt_q;   // read interrupt request threshold register
328	    irqs_d      = irqs_q;    // interrupt request status register
329	    irqen_d     = irqen_q;   // interrupt request enable register
330	    update_regs = 1'b0;      // register update enable signal
331	    // MBOX FIFO control signals
332	    mbox_w_push_o  = 1'b0;
333	    mbox_w_flush_o = 1'b0;
334	    mbox_r_pop_o   = 1'b0;
335	    mbox_r_flush_o = 1'b0;
336	    // clear the edge triggered irq register if it is instantiated
337	    clear_irq_o    = 1'b0;
338	
339	    // -------------------------------------------
340	    // Set the read and write interrupt FF (irqs), when the threshold triggers
341	    // -------------------------------------------
342	    // strict threshold interrupt these fields get cleared by acknowledge on write onto the register
343	    // read trigger, see status_q above
344	    if (!irqs_q[1] && status_q[3]) begin
345	      irqs_d[1]   = 1'b1;
346	      update_regs = 1'b1;
347	    end
348	    // write trigger, see status_q above
349	    if (!irqs_q[0] && status_q[2]) begin
350	      irqs_d[0]   = 1'b1;
351	      update_regs = 1'b1;
352	    end
353	
354	    // -------------------------------------------
355	    // Read registers
356	    // -------------------------------------------
357	    // The logic of the read and write channels have to be in the same `always_comb` block.
358	    // The reason is that the error register could be cleared in the same cycle as a read from
359	    // the mailbox FIFO generates a new error. In this case the error is NOT cleared. Instead
360	    // it will generate a new irq when it is edge triggered, or the level will stay at its
361	    // active state.
362	
363	    // Check if there is a pending read request on the slave port.
364	    if (slv_req_i.ar_valid) begin
365	      // set the right read channel output depending on the address decoding
366	      if (dec_r_valid) begin
367	        // when decode not valid, send the default slaveerror
368	        // read the right register when the transfer happens and decode is valid
369	        unique case (reg_e'(r_reg_idx))
370	          MBOXW: r_chan = '{data: data_t'( 32'hFEEDC0DE ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
371	          MBOXR: begin
372	            if (!mbox_r_empty_i) begin
373	              r_chan       = '{data: data_t'( mbox_r_data_i ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
374	              mbox_r_pop_o = 1'b1;
375	            end else begin
376	              // read mailbox is empty, set the read error Flip flop and respond with error
377	              r_chan      = '{data: data_t'( 32'hFEEDDEAD ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR};
378	              error_d[0]  = 1'b1;
379	              irqs_d[2]   = 1'b1;
380	              update_regs = 1'b1;
381	            end
382	          end
383	          STATUS: r_chan = '{data: data_t'( status_q ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
384	          ERROR: begin // clear the error register
385	            r_chan      = '{data: data_t'( error_q  ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
386	            error_d     = '0;
387	            update_regs = 1'b1;
388	          end
389	          WIRQT: r_chan = '{data: data_t'( wirqt_q  ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
390	          RIRQT: r_chan = '{data: data_t'( rirqt_q  ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
391	          IRQS:  r_chan = '{data: data_t'( irqs_q   ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
392	          IRQEN: r_chan = '{data: data_t'( irqen_q  ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
393	          IRQP:  r_chan = '{data: data_t'( irqp_q   ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
394	          CTRL:  r_chan = '{data: data_t'( ctrl_q   ), resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
395	          default: /*do nothing*/;
396	        endcase
397	      end
398	      r_valid = 1'b1;
399	      if (r_ready) begin
400	        slv_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b1;
401	      end
402	    end // read register
403	
404	    // -------------------------------------------
405	    // Write registers
406	    // -------------------------------------------
407	    // Wait for control and write data to be valid.
408	    if (slv_req_i.aw_valid && slv_req_i.w_valid) begin
409	      // Can do the handshake here as the b response goes into a spill register with latency one.
410	      // Without the B spill register, the B channel would violate the AXI stable requirement.
411	      b_valid = 1'b1;
412	      if (b_ready) begin
413	        // write to the register if required
414	        if (dec_w_valid) begin
415	          unique case (reg_e'(w_reg_idx))
416	            MBOXW:  begin
417	              if (!mbox_w_full_i) begin
418	                mbox_w_push_o = 1'b1;
419	                b_chan        = '{resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
420	              end else begin
421	                // response with default error and set the error FF
422	                error_d[1]  = 1'b1;
423	                irqs_d[2]   = 1'b1;
424	                update_regs = 1'b1;
425	              end
426	            end
427	            // MBOXR:  read only
428	            // STATUS: read only
429	            // ERROR:  read only
430	            WIRQT:  begin
431	              for (int unsigned i = 0; i < AxiDataWidth/8; i++) begin
432	                wirqt_d[i*8+:8] = slv_req_i.w.strb[i] ? slv_req_i.w.data[i*8+:8] : wirqt_d[i*8+:8];
433	              end
434	              if (wirqt_d >= data_t'(MailboxDepth)) begin
435	                // the `-1` is to have the interrupt fireing when the FIFO is comletely full
436	                wirqt_d = data_t'(MailboxDepth) - data_t'(32'd1); // Threshold to maximal value
437	              end
438	              update_regs = 1'b1;
439	              b_chan      = '{resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
440	            end
441	            RIRQT:  begin
442	              for (int unsigned i = 0; i < AxiDataWidth/8; i++) begin
443	                rirqt_d[i*8+:8] = slv_req_i.w.strb[i] ? slv_req_i.w.data[i*8+:8] : rirqt_d[i*8+:8];
444	              end
445	              if (rirqt_d >= data_t'(MailboxDepth)) begin
446	              // Threshold to maximal value, minus two to prevent overflow in usage
447	                rirqt_d = data_t'(MailboxDepth) - data_t'(32'd1);
448	              end
449	              update_regs = 1'b1;
450	              b_chan      = '{resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
451	            end
452	            IRQS:   begin
453	              // Acknowledge and clear the register by asserting the respective one
454	              if (slv_req_i.w.strb[0]) begin
455	                // *_d signal is set in the beginning of this process, prevent accidental
456	                // overwrite of not acknowledged irq
457	                irqs_d[2]   = slv_req_i.w.data[2] ? 1'b0 : irqs_d[2]; // Error irq status
458	                irqs_d[1]   = slv_req_i.w.data[1] ? 1'b0 : irqs_d[1]; // Read  irq status
459	                irqs_d[0]   = slv_req_i.w.data[0] ? 1'b0 : irqs_d[0]; // Write irq status
460	                clear_irq_o = 1'b1;
461	                update_regs = 1'b1;
462	              end
463	              b_chan = '{resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
464	            end
465	            IRQEN:  begin
466	              if (slv_req_i.w.strb[0]) begin
467	                irqen_d[2:0]  = slv_req_i.w.data[2:0]; // set the irq enable bits
468	                update_regs = 1'b1;
469	              end
470	              b_chan = '{resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
471	            end
472	            // IRQP: read only
473	            CTRL:   begin
474	              if (slv_req_i.w.strb[0]) begin
475	                mbox_r_flush_o = slv_req_i.w.data[1]; // Flush read  FIFO
476	                mbox_w_flush_o = slv_req_i.w.data[0]; // Flush write FIFO
477	              end
478	              b_chan = '{resp: axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY};
479	            end
480	            default : /* use default b_chan */;
481	          endcase
482	        end
483	        slv_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
484	        slv_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b1;
485	      end // if (b_ready): Does not violate AXI spec, because the ready comes from an internal
486	          // spill register and does not propagate the ready from the b channel.
487	    end // write register
488	  end
489	
490	  // address decoder and response FIFOs for the LITE channel, the port can take a new transaction if
491	  // these FIFOs are not full, not fall through to prevent combinational paths to the return path
492	  addr_decode #(
493	    .NoIndices( NoRegs ),
494	    .NoRules  ( NoRegs ),
495	    .addr_t   ( addr_t ),
496	    .rule_t   ( rule_t )
497	  ) i_waddr_decode (
498	    .addr_i           ( slv_req_i.aw.addr ),
499	    .addr_map_i       ( addr_map          ),
500	    .idx_o            ( w_reg_idx         ),
501	    .dec_valid_o      ( dec_w_valid       ),
502	    .dec_error_o      ( /*not used*/      ),
503	    .en_default_idx_i ( 1'b0              ),
504	    .default_idx_i    ( '0                )
505	  );
506	  spill_register #(
507	    .T ( b_chan_lite_t )
508	  ) i_b_chan_outp (
509	    .clk_i,
510	    .rst_ni,
511	    .valid_i ( b_valid            ),
512	    .ready_o ( b_ready            ),
513	    .data_i  ( b_chan             ),
514	    .valid_o ( slv_resp_o.b_valid ),
515	    .ready_i ( slv_req_i.b_ready  ),
516	    .data_o  ( slv_resp_o.b       )
517	  );
518	  addr_decode #(
519	    .NoIndices( NoRegs ),
520	    .NoRules  ( NoRegs ),
521	    .addr_t   ( addr_t ),
522	    .rule_t   ( rule_t )
523	  ) i_raddr_decode (
524	    .addr_i           ( slv_req_i.ar.addr ),
525	    .addr_map_i       ( addr_map          ),
526	    .idx_o            ( r_reg_idx         ),
527	    .dec_valid_o      ( dec_r_valid       ),
528	    .dec_error_o      ( /*not used*/      ),
529	    .en_default_idx_i ( 1'b0              ),
530	    .default_idx_i    ( '0                )
531	  );
532	  spill_register #(
533	    .T ( r_chan_lite_t )
534	  ) i_r_chan_outp (
535	    .clk_i,
536	    .rst_ni,
537	    .valid_i ( r_valid            ),
538	    .ready_o ( r_ready            ),
539	    .data_i  ( r_chan             ),
540	    .valid_o ( slv_resp_o.r_valid ),
541	    .ready_i ( slv_req_i.r_ready  ),
542	    .data_o  ( slv_resp_o.r       )
543	  );
544	  // pragma translate_off
545	  `ifndef VERILATOR
546	  initial begin : proc_check_params
547	    assert (AxiAddrWidth == $bits(slv_req_i.aw.addr)) else $fatal(1, "AW AxiAddrWidth mismatch");
548	    assert (AxiDataWidth == $bits(slv_req_i.w.data))  else $fatal(1, " W AxiDataWidth mismatch");
549	    assert (AxiAddrWidth == $bits(slv_req_i.ar.addr)) else $fatal(1, "AR AxiAddrWidth mismatch");
550	    assert (AxiDataWidth == $bits(slv_resp_o.r.data)) else $fatal(1, " R AxiDataWidth mismatch");
551	  end
552	  `endif
553	  // pragma translate_on
554	endmodule
555	
556	`include "axi/assign.svh"
557	
558	module axi_lite_mailbox_intf #(
559	  parameter int unsigned MAILBOX_DEPTH  = 32'd0,
560	  parameter bit unsigned IRQ_EDGE_TRIG  = 1'b0,
561	  parameter bit unsigned IRQ_ACT_HIGH   = 1'b1,
562	  parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 32'd0,
563	  parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 32'd0,
564	  // DEPENDENT PARAMETERS, DO NOT OVERRIDE!
565	  parameter type addr_t               = logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]
566	) (
567	  input  logic         clk_i,      // Clock
568	  input  logic         rst_ni,     // Asynchronous reset active low
569	  input  logic         test_i,     // Testmode enable
570	  AXI_LITE.Slave       slv [1:0],  // slave ports [1:0]
571	  output logic   [1:0] irq_o,      // interrupt output for each port
572	  input  addr_t  [1:0] base_addr_i // base address for each port
573	);
574	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0]   data_t;
575	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0] strb_t;
576	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(aw_chan_lite_t, addr_t)
577	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(w_chan_lite_t, data_t, strb_t)
578	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_lite_t)
579	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(ar_chan_lite_t, addr_t)
580	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(r_chan_lite_t, data_t)
581	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(req_lite_t, aw_chan_lite_t, w_chan_lite_t, ar_chan_lite_t)
582	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(resp_lite_t, b_chan_lite_t, r_chan_lite_t)
583	
584	  req_lite_t  [1:0] slv_reqs;
585	  resp_lite_t [1:0] slv_resps;
586	
587	  for (genvar i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin : gen_port_assign
588	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(slv_reqs[i], slv[i])
589	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv[i], slv_resps[i])
590	  end
591	
592	  axi_lite_mailbox #(
593	    .MailboxDepth ( MAILBOX_DEPTH  ),
594	    .IrqEdgeTrig  ( IRQ_EDGE_TRIG  ),
595	    .IrqActHigh   ( IRQ_ACT_HIGH   ),
596	    .AxiAddrWidth ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
597	    .AxiDataWidth ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
598	    .req_lite_t   ( req_lite_t     ),
599	    .resp_lite_t  ( resp_lite_t    )
600	  ) i_axi_lite_mailbox (
601	    .clk_i,      // Clock
602	    .rst_ni,     // Asynchronous reset active low
603	    .test_i,     // Testmode enable
604	    // slave ports [1:0]
605	    .slv_reqs_i  ( slv_reqs  ),
606	    .slv_resps_o ( slv_resps ),
607	    .irq_o,      // interrupt output for each port
608	    .base_addr_i // base address for each port
609	  );
610	
611	  // pragma translate_off
612	  `ifndef VERILATOR
613	  initial begin
614	    assert (slv[0].AXI_ADDR_WIDTH == AXI_ADDR_WIDTH)
615	        else $fatal(1, "LITE Interface [0] AXI_ADDR_WIDTH missmatch!");
616	    assert (slv[1].AXI_ADDR_WIDTH == AXI_ADDR_WIDTH)
617	        else $fatal(1, "LITE Interface [1] AXI_ADDR_WIDTH missmatch!");
618	    assert (slv[0].AXI_DATA_WIDTH == AXI_DATA_WIDTH)
619	        else $fatal(1, "LITE Interface [0] AXI_DATA_WIDTH missmatch!");
620	    assert (slv[1].AXI_DATA_WIDTH == AXI_DATA_WIDTH)

content.content:
# AXI4-Lite Mailbox `axi_lite_mailbox` implements a hardware mailbox, where two AXI4-Lite slave ports are connected to each other over two FIFOs. Data written on port 0 is made available on the read data at port 1 and vice versa. The module features an interrupt for each port which can be enabled with the [IRQEN](#irqen-register) register. Interrupts can be configured to trigger depending on the fill levels of the read ([RIRQT](#rirqt-register)) and write ([WIRQT](#wirqt-register)) FIFO. It is further possible to trigger an interrupt on an mailbox error condition as defined by the [ERROR](#error-register) register. ## Module Parameters This table describes the parameters of the module. | Name | Type | Description | |:---------------|:---------------|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `MailboxDepth` | `int unsigned `| The depth of the FIFOs between the two slave ports, min `32'd2` | | `IrqEdgeTrig` | `bit` | Interrupts trigger mode.
[0]: level trigger
[1]: edge trigger | | `IrqActHigh` | `bit` | Interrupts polarity.
[0]: active low / falling edge
[1]: active high / rising edge | | `AxiAddrWidth` | `int unsigned` | The AXI4-Lite address width on the AW and AR channels | | `AxiDataWidth` | `int unsigned` | The AXI4-Lite data width on the W and R channels | | `req_lite_t` | `type` | In accordance with the `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_REQ_T` macro | | `resp_lite_t` | `type` | In accordance with the `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_RESP_T` macro | ## Module Ports This table describes the ports of the module. | Name | Type | Description | |:---------------|:---------------------------|:-------------------------------------------------------| | `clk_i` | `input logic` | clock | | `rst_ni` | `input logic` | asynchronous reset active low | | `test_i` | `input logic` | testmode enable | | `slv_reqs_i` | `input req_lite_t [1:0]` | requests of the two AXI4-Lite ports | | `slv_resps_o` | `output resp_lite_t [1:0]` | responses of the two AXI4-Lite ports | | `irq_o` | `output logic [1:0]` | interrupt output for each port | | `base_addr_i` | `input addr_t [1:0]` | base address for each port | ## Register Address Mapping This table describes the type of register and the respective address mapping. The address depends on the parameter `AxiDataWidth` and the respective `base_addr_i[*]` of the port associated to the index. There are two example columns given for the resulting address for a `base_addr_i[*]` of `'0` and `AxiDataWidth` of 32 and 64 bit. Each register has one of the access types `R/W = read and write`, `R = read-only` and `W = write-only`. Writes to read-only and reads from write-only registers are responded by an `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR`. | Base Address + Offset\*AxiDataWidth/8 | Address for `AxiDataWidth = 32` | Address for `AxiDataWidth = 64` | Register Name | Access Type | Default Value | Description | |:--------------------------------------|:-------------------------------:|:-------------------------------:|:---------------------------------:|:-----------:|:-------------:|:----------------------------------| | base_addr_i + 0\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x00 | 0x00 | [MBOXW](#mboxw-register) | W | N/A | Write data address | | base_addr_i + 1\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x04 | 0x08 | [MBOXR](#mboxr-register) | R | N/A | Read data address | | base_addr_i + 2\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x08 | 0x10 | [STATUS](#status-register) | R | `0x1` | Status flags of the mailbox | | base_addr_i + 3\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x0C | 0x18 | [ERROR](#error-register) | R | `0x0` | Error flags | | base_addr_i + 4\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x10 | 0x20 | [WIRQT](#wirqt-register) | R/W | `0x0` | Write data interrupt threshold | | base_addr_i + 5\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x14 | 0x28 | [RIRQT](#rirqt-register) | R/W | `0x0` | Read data interrupt threshold | | base_addr_i + 6\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x18 | 0x30 | [IRQS](#irqs-register) | R/W | `0x0` | Interrupt status register | | base_addr_i + 7\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x1C | 0x38 | [IRQEN](#irqen-register) | R/W | `0x0` | Interrupt enable register | | base_addr_i + 8\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x20 | 0x40 | [IRQP](#irqp-register) | R | `0x0` | Interrupt pending register | | base_addr_i + 9\*AxiDataWidth/8 | 0x24 | 0x48 | [CRTL](#ctrl-register) | R/W | `0x0` | Module control register | ### MBOXW Register Mailbox write register. Write here to send data to the other slave port. A interrupt request will be raised when the fill pointer of the FIFO surpasses the [WIRQT Register](#wirqt-register) (if enabled). Writes are ignored when the FIFO is full and a `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR` is returned. Additionally the corresponding bit in the [ERROR Register](#error-register) is set. ### MBOXR Register Mailbox read register. Read here to receive data from the other slave port. A interrupt request will be raised when the fill pointer of the FIFO surpasses the [RIRQT Register](#rirqt-register) (if enabled). When the FIFO is empty, the read response `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR` is returned. Additionally the corresponding bit in the [ERROR Register](#error-register) is set. ### STATUS Register Mailbox status register. This read-only register holds the current status of the mailbox. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:4` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `3` | RFIFOL | R | `1'b0` | Read FIFO level is higher than the threshold set in `RIRQT`
[0]: Write FIFO level is less or equal to the threshold.
[1]: Read FIFO level is higher than the threshold. | | `2` | WFIFOL | R | `1'b0` | Write FIFO level is higher than the threshold set in `WIRQT`
[0]: Write FIFO level is less or equal to the threshold.
[1]: Write FIFO level is higher than the threshold. | | `1` | Full | R | `1'b0` | Write FIFO is full and subsequent writes to mailbox are ignored
[0]: Space for write data.
[1]: No space for data, writes are ignored and responded with `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR`. | | `0` | Empty | R | `1'b1` | Read FIFO is empty
[0]: Data is available.
[1]: No available data, reads respond with `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR`. | ### ERROR Register Mailbox error register. This read-only register contains information if a read occurred on an empty FIFO or a write occurred on a full FIFO. This register is cleared on read. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:2` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `1` | Write Error | R | `1'b0` | Attempted write to a full mailbox.
[0]: No Error.
[1]: Mailbox write error. | | `0` | Read Error | R | `1'b1` | Attempted read from an empty mailbox
[0]: No Error.
[1]: Mailbox read error. | ### WIRQT Register Write interrupt request threshold register. When the usage pointer of the FIFO connected to the W channel exceeds this value, a write threshold IRQ is triggered and the corresponding [STATUS register](#status-register) bit is set. When a value larger than or equal to the `MailboxDepth` parameter is written to this register, it gets reduced to `MailboxDepth - 1` to ensure an IRQ is triggered when the write FIFO is full. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:-------------------------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:--------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:$clog2(MailboxDepth)` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `$clog2(MailboxDepth)-1:0` | `WIRQT` | R/W | `'0` | Write IRQ threshold level | ### RIRQT Register Read interrupt request threshold register. When the fill pointer of the FIFO connected to the R channel exceeds this value, a read threshold IRQ is triggered and the corresponding [STATUS register](#status-register) bit is set. When a value larger than or equal to the `MailboxDepth` parameter is written to this register, it gets reduced to `MailboxDepth - 1` to ensure an IRQ is triggered when the read FIFO is full. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:-------------------------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:$clog2(MailboxDepth)` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `$clog2(MailboxDepth)-1:0` | `RIRQT` | R/W | `'0` | Read IRQ threshold level | ### IRQS Register Interrupt request status register. This register holds the current interrupt status of this slave port. There are three types of interrupts which can be enabled in the [IRQEN register](#irqen-register). The bits inside this register are sticky and get set when the trigger condition is fulfilled. This register has to be cleared explicitly by acknowledging the interrupt request status described below. This register will also get updated when the respective interrupt is not enabled. * `EIRQ`: Error interrupt request, is set to high when there was a read from an empty mailbox or a write to a full mailbox. * `RTIRQ`: Read threshold interrupt request, is set to high when the fill pointer of the FIFO connected to the R channel is higher than the threshold set in `RIRQT`. * `WTIRQ`: Write threshold interrupt request, is set to high when the fill pointer of the FIFO connected to the W channel is higher than the threshold set in `WIRQT`. To acknowledge an interrupt request write a `1'b1` to the corresponding bit described in following table. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:3` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `2` | `EIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | On read:
[0]: No interrupt request
[1]: Error on mailbox access
On write:
[0]: No acknowledge
[1]: Acknowledge and clear interrupt request | | `1` | `RTIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | On read:
[0]: No interrupt request
[1]: Usage level threshold in read mailbox exceeded
On write:
[0]: No acknowledge
[1]: Acknowledge and clear interrupt request | | `0` | `WTIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | On read:
[0]: No interrupt request
[1]: Usage level threshold in write mailbox exceeded
On write:
[0]: No acknowledge
[1]: Acknowledge and clear interrupt request | ### IRQEN Register Interrupt request enable register. Here the interrupts from [IRQS](#irqs-register) can be enabled by setting the corresponding bit in the following table. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:3` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `2` | `EIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | [0]: Error IRQ disabled
[1]: Error IRQ enabled | | `1` | `RTIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | [0]: Read threshold IRQ disabled
[1]: Read threshold IRQ enabled | | `0` | `WTIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | [0]: Write threshold IRQ disabled
[1]: Write threshold IRQ enabled | ### IRQP Register Interrupt request pending register. This read-only register holds the pending interrupts for this slave port. It is generated by the bitwise AND of the [IRQS](#irqs-register) and [IRQEN](#irqen-register) registers. An interrupt gets triggered by the OR of the bits of this register. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:--------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:3` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `2` | `EIRQ` | R | `1'b0` | [0]: No error IRQ pending
[1]: Error IRQ pending | | `1` | `RTIRQ` | R | `1'b0` | [0]: No read threshold IRQ pending
[1]: Read threshold IRQ pending | | `0` | `WTIRQ` | R | `1'b0` | [0]: No write threshold IRQ pending
[1]: Write threshold IRQ pending | ### CTRL Register Mailbox control register. Here the FIFOs can be cleared from each interface. The flush signal of each FIFO is the OR combination of the respective bit of this register at each slave port. On register write, the FIFO is cleared and the register is reset. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:---------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:2` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `1` | `RTIRQ` | W | `1'b0` | Flush the read FIFO for this port | | `0` | `WTIRQ` | W | `1'b0` | Flush the write FIFO for this port | === GIT HEAD === e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407

Data written on port 0 is made available on the read data at port 1 and vice versa. The module features an interrupt for each port which can be enabled with the [IRQEN](#irqen-register) register. Interrupts can be configured to trigger depending on the fill levels of the read ([RIRQT](#rirqt-register)) and write ([WIRQT](#wirqt-register)) FIFO. It is further possible to trigger an interrupt on an mailbox error condition as defined by the [ERROR](#error-register) register. ## Module Parameters This table describes the parameters of the module. | Name | Type | Description | |:---------------|:---------------|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `MailboxDepth` | `int unsigned `| The depth of the FIFOs between the two slave ports, min `32'd2` | | `IrqEdgeTrig` | `bit` | Interrupts trigger mode.
[0]: level trigger

Write here to send data to the other slave port. A interrupt request will be raised when the fill pointer of the FIFO surpasses the [WIRQT Register](#wirqt-register) (if enabled). Writes are ignored when the FIFO is full and a `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR` is returned. Additionally the corresponding bit in the [ERROR Register](#error-register) is set. ### MBOXR Register Mailbox read register. Read here to receive data from the other slave port. A interrupt request will be raised when the fill pointer of the FIFO surpasses the [RIRQT Register](#rirqt-register) (if enabled). When the FIFO is empty, the read response `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR` is returned. Additionally the corresponding bit in the [ERROR Register](#error-register) is set. ### STATUS Register Mailbox status register. This read-only register holds the current status of the mailbox. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:4` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `3` | RFIFOL | R | `1'b0` | Read FIFO level is higher than the threshold set in `RIRQT`

[0]: Write FIFO level is less or equal to the threshold.

[0]: Space for write data.
[1]: No space for data, writes are ignored and responded with `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR`. | | `0` | Empty | R | `1'b1` | Read FIFO is empty
[0]: Data is available.
[1]: No available data, reads respond with `axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR`. | ### ERROR Register Mailbox error register. This read-only register contains information if a read occurred on an empty FIFO or a write occurred on a full FIFO. This register is cleared on read. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:2` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `1` | Write Error | R | `1'b0` | Attempted write to a full mailbox.
[0]: No Error.
[1]: Mailbox write error. | | `0` | Read Error | R | `1'b1` | Attempted read from an empty mailbox
[0]: No Error.
[1]: Mailbox read error. | ### WIRQT Register Write interrupt request threshold register. When the usage pointer of the FIFO connected to the W channel exceeds this value, a write threshold IRQ is triggered and the corresponding [STATUS register](#status-register) bit is set. When a value larger than or equal to the `MailboxDepth` parameter is written to this register, it gets reduced to `MailboxDepth - 1` to ensure an IRQ is triggered when the write FIFO is full. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:-------------------------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:--------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:$clog2(MailboxDepth)` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `$clog2(MailboxDepth)-1:0` | `WIRQT` | R/W | `'0` | Write IRQ threshold level | ### RIRQT Register Read interrupt request threshold register. When the fill pointer of the FIFO connected to the R channel exceeds this value, a read threshold IRQ is triggered and the corresponding [STATUS register](#status-register) bit is set. When a value larger than or equal to the `MailboxDepth` parameter is written to this register, it gets reduced to `MailboxDepth - 1` to ensure an IRQ is triggered when the read FIFO is full. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:-------------------------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:$clog2(MailboxDepth)` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `$clog2(MailboxDepth)-1:0` | `RIRQT` | R/W | `'0` | Read IRQ threshold level | ### IRQS Register Interrupt request status register. This register holds the current interrupt status of this slave port. There are three types of interrupts which can be enabled in the [IRQEN register](#irqen-register). The bits inside this register are sticky and get set when the trigger condition is fulfilled. This register has to be cleared explicitly by acknowledging the interrupt request status described below. This register will also get updated when the respective interrupt is not enabled. * `EIRQ`: Error interrupt request, is set to high when there was a read from an empty mailbox or a write to a full mailbox. * `RTIRQ`: Read threshold interrupt request, is set to high when the fill pointer of the FIFO connected to the R channel is higher than the threshold set in `RIRQT`. * `WTIRQ`: Write threshold interrupt request, is set to high when the fill pointer of the FIFO connected to the W channel is higher than the threshold set in `WIRQT`. [1]: Error on mailbox access
On write:
[0]: No acknowledge
[1]: Acknowledge and clear interrupt request | | `1` | `RTIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | On read:
[0]: No interrupt request
[1]: Usage level threshold in read mailbox exceeded
On write:

[1]: Acknowledge and clear interrupt request | | `0` | `WTIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | On read:

[1]: Usage level threshold in write mailbox exceeded
On write:

[1]: Acknowledge and clear interrupt request | ### IRQEN Register Interrupt request enable register. Here the interrupts from [IRQS](#irqs-register) can be enabled by setting the corresponding bit in the following table. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:3` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `2` | `EIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | [0]: Error IRQ disabled

[1]: Read threshold IRQ enabled | | `0` | `WTIRQ` | R/W | `1'b0` | [0]: Write threshold IRQ disabled
[1]: Write threshold IRQ enabled | ### IRQP Register Interrupt request pending register. This read-only register holds the pending interrupts for this slave port. It is generated by the bitwise AND of the [IRQS](#irqs-register) and [IRQEN](#irqen-register) registers. An interrupt gets triggered by the OR of the bits of this register. | Bit(s) | Name | Access Type | Reset Value | Description | |:------------------:|:--------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:--------------------------------------------------------------------------| | `AxiDataWidth-1:3` | Reserved | | | Reserved | | `2` | `EIRQ` | R | `1'b0` | [0]: No error IRQ pending

[1]: Read threshold IRQ pending | | `0` | `WTIRQ` | R | `1'b0` | [0]: No write threshold IRQ pending
[1]: Write threshold IRQ pending | ### CTRL Register Mailbox control register. Here the FIFOs can be cleared from each interface. The flush signal of each FIFO is the OR combination of the respective bit of this register at each slave port. # axi_lite_regs：寄存器映射

## 1. 本讲目标

学完本讲，你应当能够：

- 用 `axi_lite_regs` 把一片（若干字节）内部寄存器同时暴露成 **AXI4-Lite 从端** 和 **硬件直连线**，并理解这两条访问路径如何共存。
- 配置 **只读字节**（`AxiReadOnly`）与 **特权/安全保护**（`PrivProtOnly` / `SecuProtOnly`），并说清它们各自如何影响 B/R 响应码。
- 说清 `wr_active_o` / `rd_active_o` / `reg_load_i` / `reg_q_o` 这一组硬件侧端口的用途，以及为什么 AXI 写和硬件直装载入同一字节时需要停顿。
- 读懂 `test/tb_axi_lite_regs.sv` 的「定向 + 随机」自检结构，并能仿照它写出自己的最小验证。

本讲承接 u12-l1（AXI-Lite 接口、`AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_*` / `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN*` 宏、`axi_lite_join`）。我们会复用那里的接口外壳范式，但不再自己拼 RTL，而是剖析一个现成的、库内最常用的从端模块。

## 2. 前置知识

阅读本讲前，请确认你已了解：

- **AXI4-Lite 是 AXI4 的严格子集**：每笔事务恒为单拍（无 `len/size/burst/last`），无事务 ID、无原子操作，只保留 `addr/prot/data/strb/resp`（见 u12-l1）。
- **`prot[1:0]` 的含义**（AXI4 规范 A4.7）：`prot[0]` = 特权（privileged），`prot[1]` = 安全（secure），`prot[2]` = 指令/数据。本模块只用到低两位。
- **valid/ready 握手与 `spill_register`**：一次握手须 `valid && ready` 同高；`spill_register`（来自 `common_cells`）能在切断组合路径的同时给响应加一拍延迟（见 u4-l1、u7-l1）。
- **响应码**：`RESP_OKAY = 2'b00`、`RESP_SLVERR = 2'b10`（见 [src/axi_pkg.sv:91](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_pkg.sv#L91) 与 [src/axi_pkg.sv:97](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_pkg.sv#L97)）；`prot_t` 是 3 位类型 [src/axi_pkg.sv:54](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_pkg.sv#L54)。
- **字节选通 `wstrb`**：写事务中每个字节对应一位，`strb[i]=1` 表示要写入该字节。

一个直觉：`axi_lite_regs` 就像一个「双面寄存器堆」——一面朝着 AXI 总线（软件可见），一面朝着你的 RTL 逻辑（硬件可见）。两面都能读，写权限则被精心切分，这正是外设寄存器接口（状态寄存器、控制寄存器、只读常数表）的标准做法。

## 3. 本讲源码地图

| 文件 | 作用 |
|------|------|
| [src/axi_lite_regs.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_lite_regs.sv) | 本讲主角。定义结构体内核 `axi_lite_regs`（接受 `req_lite_t` / `resp_lite_t`）与接口外壳 `axi_lite_regs_intf`（端口是 `AXI_LITE.Slave`）。 |
| [test/tb_axi_lite_regs.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/test/tb_axi_lite_regs.sv) | 配套测试台。用随机 Lite 主端 + 定向读写 + 随机硬件直装载入 + 并发 checker 自检，是本库「定向随机验证」的典型范例。 |
| [src/axi_pkg.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_pkg.sv) | 提供 `RESP_OKAY` / `RESP_SLVERR`、`prot_t` 等常量与类型（Level 0 根包）。 |
| [src/axi_intf.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_intf.sv) | 提供 `AXI_LITE` / `AXI_LITE_DV` 接口（u12-l1 已讲）。 |

依赖关系：本讲只依赖 u12-l1。模块本身位于 Bender Level 2（依赖 `addr_decode`、`spill_register` 等 common_cells 原语）。

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 整体架构：一片字节寄存器，两条访问路径

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

`axi_lite_regs` 的本质是一组用触发器（FF）实现的、按 **字节** 寻址的寄存器阵列 `reg_q[RegNumBytes]`。它的特别之处在于这组字节同时挂在两个「面」上：

- **AXI4-Lite 面**（软件面）：通过 `axi_req_i` / `axi_resp_o` 被总线读写。
- **硬件面**（逻辑面）：通过 `reg_d_i` / `reg_load_i` / `reg_q_o` / `wr_active_o` / `rd_active_o` 直接接到周边 RTL。

这两个面不是互斥的，而是 **受控共存**：软件可写哪些字节、硬件可写哪些字节，由参数和运行期信号精细划分。模块头注释把这两个面讲得很清楚 [src/axi_lite_regs.sv:18-24](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_lite_regs.sv#L18-L24)。

一个关键设计：存储单元是 **字节粒度** 的，不是「32 位寄存器」粒度。`RegNumBytes` 直接数字节，`AxiReadOnly` 也是每字节一比特。这使得「一个 32 位寄存器里某些字节只读、某些可写」这样的配置天然支持。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

模块可看作两条相对独立的通路加一组共享寄存器：

```text
               ┌─────────────── reg_q[RegNumBytes]（共享 FF 阵列）──────────────┐
               │                                                              │
   写通路:      │   AW+W → addr_decode 找 chunk → prot 校验 → RO/load 检查      │
               │            → 写 reg_d → FFLARN 入 reg_q                       │

## 1. 本讲目标

学完本讲，你应当能够：

- 用 `axi_lite_regs` 把一片（若干字节）内部寄存器同时暴露成 **AXI4-Lite 从端** 和 **硬件直连线**，并理解这两条访问路径如何共存。
- 配置 **只读字节**（`AxiReadOnly`）与 **特权/安全保护**（`PrivProtOnly` / `SecuProtOnly`），并说清它们各自如何影响 B/R 响应码。
- 说清 `wr_active_o` / `rd_active_o` / `reg_load_i` / `reg_q_o` 这一组硬件侧端口的用途，以及为什么 AXI 写和硬件直装载入同一字节时需要停顿。
- 读懂 `test/tb_axi_lite_regs.sv` 的「定向 + 随机」自检结构，并能仿照它写出自己的最小验证。

本讲承接 u12-l1（AXI-Lite 接口、`AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_*` / `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN*` 宏、`axi_lite_join`）。我们会复用那里的接口外壳范式，但不再自己拼 RTL，而是剖析一个现成的、库内最常用的从端模块。

## 2. 前置知识

阅读本讲前，请确认你已了解：

- **AXI4-Lite 是 AXI4 的严格子集**：每笔事务恒为单拍（无 `len/size/burst/last`），无事务 ID、无原子操作，只保留 `addr/prot/data/strb/resp`（见 u12-l1）。
- **`prot[1:0]` 的含义**（AXI4 规范 A4.7）：`prot[0]` = 特权（privileged），`prot[1]` = 安全（secure），`prot[2]` = 指令/数据。本模块只用到低两位。
- **valid/ready 握手与 `spill_register`**：一次握手须 `valid && ready` 同高；`spill_register`（来自 `common_cells`）能在切断组合路径的同时给响应加一拍延迟（见 u4-l1、u7-l1）。
- **响应码**：`RESP_OKAY = 2'b00`、`RESP_SLVERR = 2'b10`（见 [src/axi_pkg.sv:91](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_pkg.sv#L91) 与 [src/axi_pkg.sv:97](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_pkg.sv#L97)）；`prot_t` 是 3 位类型 [src/axi_pkg.sv:54](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_pkg.sv#L54)。
- **字节选通 `wstrb`**：写事务中每个字节对应一位，`strb[i]=1` 表示要写入该字节。

一个直觉：`axi_lite_regs` 就像一个「双面寄存器堆」——一面朝着 AXI 总线（软件可见），一面朝着你的 RTL 逻辑（硬件可见）。两面都能读，写权限则被精心切分，这正是外设寄存器接口（状态寄存器、控制寄存器、只读常数表）的标准做法。

## 3. 本讲源码地图

| [src/axi_lite_regs.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_lite_regs.sv) | 本讲主角。定义结构体内核 `axi_lite_regs`（接受 `req_lite_t` / `resp_lite_t`）与接口外壳 `axi_lite_regs_intf`（端口是 `AXI_LITE.Slave`）。 |

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 整体架构：一片字节寄存器，两条访问路径

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

`axi_lite_regs` 的本质是一组用触发器（FF）实现的、按 **字节** 寻址的寄存器阵列 `reg_q[RegNumBytes]`。它的特别之处在于这组字节同时挂在两个「面」上：

- **AXI4-Lite 面**（软件面）：通过 `axi_req_i` / `axi_resp_o` 被总线读写。
- **硬件面**（逻辑面）：通过 `reg_d_i` / `reg_load_i` / `reg_q_o` / `wr_active_o` / `rd_active_o` 直接接到周边 RTL。

这两个面不是互斥的，而是 **受控共存**：软件可写哪些字节、硬件可写哪些字节，由参数和运行期信号精细划分。模块头注释把这两个面讲得很清楚 [src/axi_lite_regs.sv:18-24](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_lite_regs.sv#L18-L24)。

一个关键设计：存储单元是 **字节粒度** 的，不是「32 位寄存器」粒度。`RegNumBytes` 直接数字节，`AxiReadOnly` 也是每字节一比特。这使得「一个 32 位寄存器里某些字节只读、某些可写」这样的配置天然支持。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

模块可看作两条相对独立的通路加一组共享寄存器：

```text
               ┌─────────────── reg_q[RegNumBytes]（共享 FF 阵列）──────────────┐
               │                                                              │
   写通路:      │   AW+W → addr_decode 找 chunk → prot 校验 → RO/load 检查      │
               │            → 写 reg_d → FFLARN 入 reg_q                       │

Key behaviors:
- MBOXW write: if not full → push, RESP_OKAY; if full → error_d[1]=1, irqs_d[2]=1, RESP_SLVERR (default)
- MBOXR read: if not empty → data + pop, RESP_OKAY; if empty → data=0xFEEDDEAD, error_d[0]=1, irqs_d[2]=1, RESP_SLVERR
- MBOXW read: returns 0xFEEDC0DE
- STATUS read: returns status_q
- ERROR read: returns error_q and clears it (error_d='0)
- WIRQT write: strb-masked write, clamps to MailboxDepth-1 if >= MailboxDepth
- RIRQT write: same as WIRQT
- IRQS write: acknowledge (clears bits by writing 1), also sets clear_irq_o
- IRQEN write: sets irqen_d[2:0]
- CTRL write: bit 1 = flush read FIFO, bit 0 = flush write FIFO

Flush mechanism: each FIFO's flush is OR of both ports' flush signals

- `i_mbox_1_to_0.flush_i = w_mbox_flush[1] | r_mbox_flush[0]` (line 153)

So flushing FIFO 0 (0→1): port 0's write flush OR port 1's read flush.

### Interrupt logic
- `slv_irq[i]` = |irqp_q (OR of pending interrupts) — level triggered, active high
- Three interrupt types:
  - bit 2: EIRQ (error interrupt) — set on read-empty or write-full error
  - bit 1: RTIRQ (read threshold interrupt) — set when read FIFO usage > RIRQT
  - bit 0: WTIRQ (write threshold interrupt) — set when write FIFO usage > WIRQT
- IRQS (status): sticky, set when condition met, cleared by write-acknowledge
- IRQEN (enable): masks IRQS to form IRQP (pending)
- IRQP (pending) = IRQS & IRQEN
- irq_o = |IRQP

### usage_t and threshold comparison
- `usage_t = logic [FifoUsageWidth:0]` where `FifoUsageWidth = $clog2(MailboxDepth)`
- The MSB of usage_t is the full flag. `mbox_usage[i] = {mbox_full[i], usage_from_fifo}`
- This is one bit wider so threshold detection can detect "completely full"

For example, MailboxDepth=16: FifoUsageWidth = 4, usage_t = logic[4:0] (5 bits). The fifo usage is 4 bits (0-15), and the MSB (bit 4) is the full flag.

Let me be precise about a few technical details:

1. The threshold comparison: `mbox_r_usage_i > usage_t'(rirqt_q)`. The usage includes the full bit as MSB. The threshold register rirqt_q is data_t (full AxiDataWidth), but cast to usage_t for comparison. So only the low bits matter. When threshold written >= MailboxDepth, it's clamped to MailboxDepth-1.

Wait, the comparison uses `usage_t'(rirqt_q)`. usage_t is [FifoUsageWidth:0] = [$clog2(Depth):0]. For Depth=16, that's [4:0], 5 bits. rirqt_q is data_t. Casting data_t to usage_t truncates to low 5 bits. So if you write threshold = 15 (= MailboxDepth-1), comparison is usage > 15. usage max when full = {1, 1111} = 11111 = 31 > 15 → triggers. Good, that's why the doc says clamping to MailboxDepth-1 ensures IRQ fires when FIFO is completely full.

2. The "MSB is full flag" design — this is clever. By concatenating the full flag as the MSB of usage_t, the threshold comparison `usage > threshold` naturally handles the "completely full" case which a plain usage counter of $clog2(Depth) bits couldn't represent distinctly (since 16 needs 5 bits but a 4-bit usage tops at 15).

3. Edge vs level trigger:
- Level (default, IrqEdgeTrig=0): `irq_o[i] = IrqActHigh ? slv_irq[i] : ~slv_irq[i]` — directly follows the OR of pending interrupts.
- Edge (IrqEdgeTrig=1): a register irq_q latches; on rising edge of slv_irq (and not being cleared), it pulses. clear_irq clears the latch.

4. The flush: CTRL register bit 0 = flush write FIFO, bit 1 = flush read FIFO. Each FIFO is flushed by OR of the write-flush from one port and the read-flush from the other (since they're shared between the two ports — FIFO 0 is port0-write/port1-read).

Let me make sure I have the FIFO ownership right for flush:

  - r_mbox_flush[1] = port 1's CTRL read-flush (read FIFO from port 1's perspective = FIFO 0)
  - ✓ Both ports can flush their shared FIFO.
- FIFO 1 (i_mbox_1_to_0): port 1 writes, port 0 reads.
  - `flush_i = w_mbox_flush[1] | r_mbox_flush[0]` (line 153)
  - ✓

Good. Now I'm confident. Let me also double check the "from each port's perspective" naming:
- For port i: "write FIFO" = the FIFO it pushes to (FIFO i); "read FIFO" = the FIFO it pops from (FIFO 1-i).
- Port 0: write FIFO = FIFO 0, read FIFO = FIFO 1.
- Port 1: write FIFO = FIFO 1, read FIFO = FIFO 0.

So `w_mbox_flush[i]` flushes FIFO i, `r_mbox_flush[i]` flushes FIFO (1-i). Confirmed by the OR expressions.
